app.title: ZeroClaw Dockyard
app.heading: Dockyard WebUI
app.description: Local control surface for structured agent configuration and Docker API orchestration.

images.title: Images
images.subtitle: Inspect ZeroClaw runtime images, pull the official image, and build local advanced variants.
images.buildHint: Building images requires Docker socket proxy BUILD access. Copy .env.example to .env, keep DOCKER_SOCKET_PROXY_BUILD=1, then restart docker compose before building.

dashboard.subtitle: Agent runtime state, quick controls, and recent logs.

history.subtitle: Recent manager operations, with secrets redacted.

agents.empty: Create or select an agent to edit its settings.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Manage canonical SKILL.md bundles, support files, and agent skill settings.
skills.empty: Create or select a skill bundle.
skills.bundleSkillsHelp: Skills are stored as SKILL.md plus scripts/, references/, and assets/ under the bundle directory.
skills.noSkills: No skills in this bundle yet.
skills.runtimeHelp: Global skill loading, registry, creation, and improvement settings rendered into agent runtime config.
skills.bundleSettingsHelp: Configure the selected bundle alias, directory, and optional include/exclude filters.
skills.supportFilesHelp: Choose a bundle and skill, then edit files under references/, scripts/, or assets/.
skills.supportTypeHelp: Files shown here live under the selected support directory for the current skill.
skills.scriptsDisabledNotice: Script files are disabled globally. Enabling this affects every skill bundle and agent runtime.
skills.addTextFileSubtitle: Create a UTF-8 text file under the selected support directory.
skills.uploadFileSubtitle: Upload a local text file into the selected support directory.
skills.uploadTextOnlyNotice: Uploads preserve the original file bytes. Text files can be edited after upload; binary files can be downloaded or replaced.

prompts.title: Prompt Templates
prompts.subtitle: Edit reusable workspace prompt files and duplicate templates.
prompts.empty: Create or select a prompt template.
prompts.orderSummary: Official ZeroClaw prompt files and their read order
prompts.orderSystem: Normal channel turns read workspace files from the workspace root in the source-defined bootstrap order. The file tabs below are ordered to match that read order where applicable. HEARTBEAT.md also lives at the workspace root, but is for the heartbeat worker only and is not injected into normal channel prompts. PROACTIVE.md is a ZeroClaw Dockyard optional-service convention, not an official ZeroClaw prompt file.
prompts.orderFlow:
normal channel system prompt:
  tools/safety/skills/workspace
  + AGENTS.md
  + SOUL.md
  + TOOLS.md
  + IDENTITY.md
  + USER.md
  + BOOTSTRAP.md if it exists
  + MEMORY.md when memory injection is enabled
  + current date/runtime/channel capabilities

heartbeat worker:
  reads HEARTBEAT.md from the workspace root
  parses lines that start with "- " as periodic tasks
  supports [high], [medium], [low], [paused], [completed] metadata

optional proactive service:
  may read PROACTIVE.md in this project
  gives each agent its own outbound rhythm and judgment
  not read automatically by official ZeroClaw

custom files:
  written to the workspace root only
  not read automatically unless an official file tells the agent to use them
prompts.customNote: The default contents here mirror ZeroClaw's personality template preset. Placeholders such as {agent}, {user}, {tz}, and {comm_style} are starter text that you can edit before applying a template.
prompts.officialFileHelp: This file is injected into the normal channel system prompt in the numbered order shown on its tab.
prompts.heartbeatFileHelp: This file is read by the heartbeat worker from the workspace root and is not injected into normal channel prompts.
prompts.optionalServiceFileHelp: This project convention is for the optional proactive service; official ZeroClaw will not read it automatically.
prompts.customFileHelp: This file is written to the workspace root only; reference it from an official file to make it effective.
prompts.aiFillSubtitle: Generate all files in this template using one LLM profile. Review the result, then save manually.
prompts.aiDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe who this agent is, what it does, preferences, relationship to the user, tone, boundaries, and useful context.
prompts.aiUseReferences: Send selected current files to AI as reference context

export.title: Config Export
export.subtitle: Inspect primary config, resolved config, and generated output preview.

messages.aiFillGenerated: AI generated the template draft. Review the files and click Save to persist it.
messages.validationFailed: Validation failed. Review the details below.
messages.syncedToRuntime: Local files synced to runtime volume.
messages.syncedFromRuntime: Runtime volume synced back to local files.
messages.resetMatrixState: Matrix E2EE state reset and device ID rotated.
messages.resetMatrixRunning: Agent container is running. Stop it before resetting Matrix state.
messages.invalidJsonField: {field} is not valid JSON. Please check it again.
messages.invalidNumberField: {field} must be a valid number. Please check it again.
messages.invalidMinNumberField: {field} must be greater than or equal to {min}.
messages.invalidMaxNumberField: {field} must be less than or equal to {max}.
messages.invalidUrlField: {field} must be a valid http/https URL. Please check it again.
messages.requiredField: {field} is required. Please fill it in.
messages.invalidTemplateFile: Template file names must be plain workspace file names using letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or hyphen.
messages.invalidSupportFileName: Support file names must include a file extension and stay inside the selected support file type.
messages.binaryFileSelected: Binary file selected. Use Download to inspect it, or upload a replacement.
messages.pathUnavailable: No local path is available for this item.

confirm.deleteAgent: Delete this agent? This cannot be undone.
confirm.deleteProfile: Delete this profile? Assigned agents may need updates.
confirm.resetMatrixState: This will delete the local Matrix encryption state and generate a new device ID for this Matrix configuration. Other agents using the same Matrix account or configuration may be affected. Continue?
confirm.deleteSkillBundle: Delete this skill bundle config? Skill files on disk are not removed.
confirm.buildPythonImage: This will build a local ZeroClaw image with Python installed. Docker build can execute commands as root through the Docker daemon. Continue?
confirm.buildRootImage: This will build a local ZeroClaw image that runs as root. Root containers can write files as root and increase runtime risk. Continue?

agent.id: Manager-local agent ID. Used for generated container labels, instance directories, and API routes.
agent.hostPort: Loopback port on the host that maps to this agent's ZeroClaw gateway.
agent.llmProfile: Required model provider profile. Edit model credentials and provider behavior in LLM Profiles.
agent.visionProfile: Optional vision-capable model profile for image attachments. Leave empty to disable the dedicated vision route for this agent.
agent.matrixProfile: Required Matrix profile. Matrix user, device, password, token, rooms, and behavior are controlled there.
agent.promptTemplate: Workspace prompt template to apply to this agent's instance directory.
agent.name: Optional display/container name. Leave blank to use the ID.
agent.image: ZeroClaw Docker image for this agent. Defaults to the manager's current ZeroClaw image.
agent.mcpProfile: Optional MCP gateway profile assigned to this agent.
agent.skillBundles: Skill bundle aliases to load for this agent, one per line. Create and edit bundles in Skills.
agent.externalPeers: Required per-agent Matrix peer list used for the generated peer group and send_message_to_peer authorization. Matrix identity and credentials come from the Matrix profile.
agent.templateApplyMode: Controls how prompt template files are written when Apply template is clicked.
agent.environment: Advanced explicit environment variables merged into the generated agent container environment.
agent.proactiveEnabled: Create a per-agent proactive sidecar container that wakes this agent on its own schedule.
agent.proactiveTarget: Matrix peer target for send_message_to_peer. Leave blank to use the first External peers entry.
agent.proactiveRandomMinMinutes: Minimum random delay before the next proactive wake.
agent.proactiveRandomMaxMinutes: Maximum random delay before the next proactive wake.
agent.proactivePollSeconds: How often the sidecar checks whether the next wake time is due. Minimum is 30 seconds.
agent.proactiveQuietHours: Local quiet-hour range like 23-8. Leave blank to disable quiet hours.
agent.proactiveTimezone: Timezone used for quiet hours, for example Asia/Tokyo.
agent.proactiveChannel: Matrix channel alias passed to send_message_to_peer. Default is matrix.home.
agent.proactiveAgentUrl: Advanced full gateway URL. Leave blank to use this agent's host port and /webhook?agent=main automatically.
agent.proactivePrompt: Optional override for the wake prompt. Leave blank to use the built-in PROACTIVE.md-oriented prompt.

matrix.id: Reusable profile ID selected by agents.
matrix.homeserver: Matrix homeserver URL, such as https://matrix.org or your Synapse server.
matrix.userId: Bot account MXID. Required for password login and useful for identity checks.
matrix.deviceId: Stable Matrix device ID. Keep it stable for token-based E2EE sessions; password login can usually leave it blank.
matrix.password: Recommended login credential. With user ID, ZeroClaw can log in and recover corrupted Matrix state automatically.
matrix.recoveryKey: E2EE secure backup key. Lets ZeroClaw restore room keys and cross-sign the bot device on startup.
matrix.allowedRooms: Canonical room IDs accepted for inbound messages. Empty allows all rooms the bot has joined; use !room:server, not #aliases.
matrix.mentionOnly: In group rooms, respond only when the bot is mentioned. Direct messages are still processed.
matrix.replyInThread: Send replies inside Matrix threads rooted at the triggering message.
matrix.ackReactions: React while processing and when done, if enabled.
matrix.interruptOnNewMessage: Cancel an in-flight answer when a newer message arrives for the same conversation.
matrix.streamMode: off sends one final reply, partial edits a draft, multi_message splits paragraphs into separate messages.
matrix.multiMessageDelayMs: Delay between paragraph messages when stream mode is multi_message. Default is 800 ms.
matrix.channelDebounceMs: Global channel debounce delay before dispatching incoming messages. Manager default is 3000 ms.
matrix.accessToken: Alternative token-based login. Prefer user ID plus password unless you must reuse an existing token.
matrix.draftUpdateIntervalMs: Minimum edit interval for partial streaming. Native default is 1500 ms.
matrix.approvalTimeoutSecs: How long Matrix approval prompts wait before auto-denying always-ask tools.
matrix.excludedTools: Tool names to hide from the model when responding through Matrix, one per line.
matrix.replyMinIntervalSecs: Outbound pacing floor per Matrix recipient. 0 disables pacing.
matrix.replyQueueDepthMax: Maximum queued outbound replies when pacing is active. 0 uses the runtime default.
matrix.hostIp: Host IP exposed to agent containers as matrix-host; usually inherited from Docker settings.

vision.provider: ZeroClaw provider family for the vision route, such as openai, gemini, ollama, or custom.
vision.providerAlias: Alias used in the generated providers.models section. Together with provider family this forms the vision_model_provider ref.
vision.baseUrl: HTTP endpoint for the vision provider. For OpenAI-compatible providers, include the API prefix such as /v1 when required.
vision.model: Provider-local model ID used when ZeroClaw routes image turns to the vision provider.
vision.wireApi: Request protocol used by the vision provider.
vision.timeout: Request timeout for the vision provider.
vision.apiKey: Credential for the vision provider. Leave blank only for local providers or external auth flows.
vision.maxImages: Maximum number of image attachments preserved in one provider request. ZeroClaw clamps this to 1-16.
vision.maxImageSizeMb: Maximum image payload size before base64 encoding. ZeroClaw clamps this to 1-20 MB.
vision.maxImageTurns: How many recent conversation turns keep image data. Use 0 to disable age trimming.
vision.allowRemoteFetch: Allow ZeroClaw to fetch http/https image URLs. Local files and Matrix attachments do not require this.

skills.allowScripts: Allow script-like files in skill scripts/. Keep this disabled unless you trust the skill contents.
skills.openSkillsEnabled: Enable ZeroClaw's open-skills repository sync path for community skills.
skills.promptInjectionMode: full injects full skill instructions; compact injects smaller metadata and relies on on-demand reading.
skills.registryUrl: Git repository used by ZeroClaw for bare-name skill installs.
skills.extraRegistries: Additional skill registries as JSON. Each entry can include name, url, kind, and enabled.
skills.skillCreationEnabled: Allow ZeroClaw to create new skills automatically after successful multi-step tasks.
skills.skillCreationMaxSkills: Maximum number of automatically created skills to keep.
skills.skillCreationSimilarity: Deduplication threshold for automatic skill creation. Higher values allow more similar skills.
skills.installSuggestionsEnabled: Allow ZeroClaw to suggest installable skills when a prompt appears to need one.
skills.skillImprovementEnabled: Allow the background review fork to improve skills after they are used.
skills.skillImprovementCooldown: Minimum seconds between automatic reviews for the same skill.
skills.skillImprovementNudge: Tool-call iteration count that can trigger a background skill review. 0 disables iteration nudges.
skills.skillImprovementMaxReview: Maximum tool-call iterations allowed inside one background skill review.
skills.bundleId: Bundle alias selected by agents. Use letters, numbers, underscore, or hyphen.
skills.bundleDirectory: Directory containing this bundle's skill folders. Relative paths resolve from the project root and must stay inside shared/.
skills.includeSkills: Optional allow-list of skill directory names, one per line. Empty includes all skills in the bundle directory.
skills.excludeSkills: Skill directory names to remove from this bundle, one per line. Exclude wins over include.
skills.skillName: Skill directory name and frontmatter name. Use a short stable slug.
skills.description: One-line description shown to the model and in lists. Required by the Agent Skills format.
skills.version: Optional skill version written into SKILL.md frontmatter.
skills.author: Optional author written into SKILL.md frontmatter.
skills.license: Optional license written into SKILL.md frontmatter.
skills.category: Optional category written into SKILL.md frontmatter.
skills.tags: Skill tags, one per line. The slash tag can expose a skill as a slash command in supported channels.
skills.body: Markdown instructions the agent reads when using this skill.
skills.supportFile: Existing support file to load into the editor.
skills.supportFilePath: Relative path under references/, scripts/, or assets/. scripts/ requires Allow skill scripts.
skills.supportFileType: Top-level support directory for this file.
skills.supportFileName: File name or nested path under the selected type. Include a file extension, such as notes.md.
skills.supportFileContent: Content written to the support file path.

resources.title: Docker Resources
resources.subtitle: Read-only audit of containers, volumes, and networks that are expected, orphaned, legacy-looking, or conflicting.
resources.expectedErrors: Some configured agents could not be evaluated for expected Docker resources.
resources.name: Name
resources.aboutSummary: Open for the exact matching rules.
resources.expectedHelp: The current manager configuration expects this resource name, and Docker labels mark it as manager-owned.
resources.conflictsHelp: The current configuration expects this resource name, but the resource is missing manager ownership labels. This often means an older version created it, so review before modifying it.
resources.orphansHelp: Docker labels mark this resource as manager-owned, but the current configuration no longer expects it.
resources.untrackedHelp: The resource name contains zeroclaw, but it is not expected by the current configuration and has no manager ownership label. It may belong to another local setup.
resources.reviewedHelp: Mark reviewed only records a local decision in config/generated. It does not change Docker labels, rename resources, or migrate data.
resources.deleteTypeName: Type the resource name to confirm deletion

resourceDeleteWarnings.container: Deleting a container stops and removes that container, but it does not remove its volumes.
resourceDeleteWarnings.volume: Deleting a volume can permanently remove runtime data, workspace files, Matrix encryption state, and unsynced changes.
resourceDeleteWarnings.network: Deleting a network can disconnect containers that still depend on it.
resourceDeleteWarnings.default: Deleting a Docker resource cannot be undone.

resourceClassifications.managed_orphan: Managed but not in current config
